metadata.match_reason: PromptQL is Hasura's flagship enterprise GenAI/RAG platform on Hasura DDN; team hires across LLM agents, retrieval, runtime infra  â€” Amit's Milvus/Qdrant/RAG/Python is exact match.
metadata.notes: PromptQL is Hasura's strategic 2025-26 bet. Bengaluru + SF.  Watch Freshteam closely for mid-level backfills.

research_summary: PromptQL (formerly Hasura) is a $1B+ enterprise AI company building accurate, steer-able AI on top of enterprise data via Hasura DDN. The engineering team is split Bengaluru/SF. The open role targets Python engineers who can work across AI assistant, data engine, and runtime infra layers. Recent signals: Forbes 'MIT Says 95% Enterprise AI Fail' coverage, $900/hr consulting practice launch, Berkeley EPIC Lab DAB benchmark collaboration, and PromptQL Program API (April 2026). Amit's RAG/Milvus/LLM work is a direct technical match. 7 contacts captured: 2 Tier 1 (Director of Eng, Staff Engineer), 3 Tier 3 ICs, 2 Tier 4 HR.
jd_full_text:
Software Engineer - PromptQL AI

About PromptQL
PromptQL is redefining how AI systems should access data in a business context with an emphasis on visibility, steer-ability, and repeatability. Building on the foundation of Hasura's Data Delivery Network (DDN), PromptQL is our flagship product. AI can't be trusted if it's confidently wrong. At PromptQL, we're on a mission to deliver reasoning AI that learns from enterprise data, signals its confidence, and earns trust through accuracy.

About the Role
As part of a small, high-impact team working directly with PromptQL leadership, you'll help shape the product direction and deliver core functionality across AI, data, and infrastructure layers.

What You'll Do
- Analyze the AI + data landscape to influence the evolution of PromptQL
- Translate feedback from sales and GTM teams into actionable product features
- Rapidly design and implement features across the PromptQL AI assistant, the PromptQL data engine, and the PromptQL runtime infrastructure

What We're Looking For
- Ability to understand and work across complex systems end-to-end
- Strong foundation in software engineering principles and adaptability across domains
- Proficiency in Python (PromptQL programs are Python; Primitives for classify, summarize, extract)
- Experience working with LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic) -- big plus
- Experience with distributed data systems, query engines, or observability infrastructure

Tech Stack
- Python (primary programming language for PromptQL programs)
- LLM providers: Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT models
- Hasura DDN connectors for enterprise data integration
- Runtime infrastructure

Location: Bengaluru, India
Compensation: 28-40 LPA
tailoring_notes: Summary rewritten to lead with production AI data retrieval and Milvus RAG -- JD is PromptQL: AI systems that access data with visibility and repeatability. Skills reordered: Vector DBs and Retrieval first (Milvus, BGE-M3, embeddings -- core PromptQL concern), LLMs 2nd, Languages 3rd, Infrastructure 4th; Hardware/IoT dropped. IIT Delhi trimmed to 1 bullet. Projects: WeepScope replaced with SAP Analytics Bot (NL-to-data-query work maps directly to PromptQL mission).

contacts.research_hook: Posted 1 month ago about PromptQL shifting teams from Slack conversations to autonomous execution -- 'work is already in motion' -- and reposted the Berkeley EPIC Data Lab DAB benchmark collaboration showing most LLMs fail on enterprise data
contacts.subject: saw your PromptQL execution post last month
contacts.content:
Hi Manushi,

Your LinkedIn post last month about PromptQL shifting from tagging people to get something moving to threads where work is already in motion caught my attention. You reposted the Berkeley EPIC Lab DAB benchmark the same day. The accuracy problem in enterprise AI lives in context and retrieval architecture, not in the models themselves.

I've spent two years on that exact problem. At Vivansh I built a permission-aware RAG chatbot on Milvus with 2-stage retrieval: 250+ per-query access control checks, 10K+ content chunks, 80% MRR with GPT-4o. At Silver Touch I rebuilt production RAG infrastructure from FAISS to Milvus, 45% to 95% MRR, after testing 6 embedding models on production-scale data.

If PromptQL's Bengaluru team is hiring, I'd appreciate 15 minutes to hear how you're thinking about the retrieval layer. Tuesday or Thursday next week works.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | github.com/amit-gohel | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: saw your PromptQL execution post last month
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Manushi,

Following up in case this got buried. PromptQL's May post on 150 million records and zero wait time is a good illustration of the accuracy-at-scale problem from the enterprise side. The retrieval architecture decisions in that kind of setup are exactly what I've been working on.

If the timing isn't right, no worries. Happy to circle back next quarter.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: saw your PromptQL execution post last month
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Manushi,

Last note from me. If the Bengaluru engineering team isn't actively hiring right now, totally understood. Would appreciate staying on your radar for when it does come up.

Good luck with the Berkeley collaboration rollout.

Amit

contacts.contact_role: Staff Engineer - Infrastructure, Observability, Distributed Databases
contacts.research_hook: Reposted Forbes article 'MIT Says 95% Of Enterprise AI Fail -- Here's What The 5% Are Doing Right' featuring PromptQL, and reposted the AI-as-co-worker vs AI-as-calculator thread that aligns with PromptQL's multiplayer-AI-with-a-wiki vision
contacts.subject: infra angle on PromptQL's retrieval pipeline
contacts.content:
Hi Karthik,

Came across your repost of the Forbes piece on enterprise AI failure last month. The core argument tracks with what I've seen in production: the accuracy gap in RAG at scale sits in infrastructure, not prompt engineering. Data access patterns, retrieval architecture, latency under concurrent load.

I've been in that gap for two years. At Silver Touch I rebuilt RAG infrastructure from FAISS to Milvus, improved MRR from 45% to 95% after testing three vector databases on production-scale data. At Vivansh I built the same kind of system with 2-stage retrieval, 250+ per-query access control checks, vLLM-served inference, running at 500+ concurrent users on EC2.

Your work on observability and distributed databases is the infrastructure layer I want to work on next. If the Bengaluru team has room for a mid-level engineer, 15 minutes on Thursday would be worth it.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | github.com/amit-gohel | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat

contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Karthik,

Following up in case this got lost. PromptQL's April piece on the three silent killers of enterprise AI (data staleness, context fragmentation, permission gaps) lines up with the retrieval pipeline problems I've been solving. The observability layer you work on is exactly the foundation that makes these diagnosable.

If the timing isn't right, no problem.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: Active open source builder at Hasura (GitHub: scriptnull), working on internal tooling that sits on top of the DDN layer -- same systems layer Amit's OCR-to-retrieval pipelines operate in
contacts.subject: your hasura tooling work on GitHub
contacts.content:
Hi Vishnu,

Went through your GitHub (scriptnull) while researching PromptQL's engineering team. The DDN tooling work caught my eye: building reliable interfaces on top of a distributed data layer is the exact kind of problem I've been working on from the application side.

At Vivansh and Silver Touch I spent two years on that stack: FastAPI backends on Milvus, LLM inference via vLLM, Pydantic-validated intermediate schemas so LLM output is actually usable downstream. Production scale. 50K+ queries per month at Silver Touch, 500+ concurrent users at Vivansh.

If PromptQL is hiring on the Bengaluru engineering side, would you be open to pointing me toward the right person? Happy to share more context.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | github.com/amit-gohel | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: your hasura tooling work on GitHub
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Vishnu,

Following up in case this got buried. One thing I didn't mention: the Selenium-based dynamic crawler I built for RAG content ingestion handles tricky UI state (tabs, dropdowns, accordions) that tooling infrastructure often needs to work around. If that's relevant to anything you're building, happy to share details.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: IIT Madras grad building on PromptQL; PromptQL Program API (April 2026 launch) is the Python-based integration layer -- maps directly to Amit's FastAPI + LLM pipeline work
contacts.subject: PromptQL Program API and Python pipeline work
contacts.content:
Hi Varun,

Was reading through PromptQL's April announcement on the Program API. The Python-function integration approach is a clean abstraction: programs calling data functions, LLM primitives layered on top. Pretty close to the architecture I've been building production RAG systems on.

At Vivansh I built a multi-model orchestration pipeline: GPT-4o for document analysis, Qwen3-Coder-480B for blueprint generation, GPT-4o-mini for JSON conversion, all in FastAPI with Pydantic-validated intermediate schemas. Before that at Silver Touch I migrated a 50K-query-per-month RAG chatbot from FAISS to Milvus, 45% to 95% MRR, after benchmarking 6 embedding models and landing on BGE-M3 plus a reranker.

If PromptQL's engineering team has room for someone with this stack, would you know the right person to talk to?

Amit
amitgohel.dev | github.com/amit-gohel | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: PromptQL Program API and Python pipeline work
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Varun,

Following up in case this got lost. One specific data point that might be relevant: on the form builder at Vivansh, GPT-4o and Claude both failed to reliably generate the platform's native JSON schema, but adding a Pydantic-validated intermediate step made it production-stable. That kind of abstraction is what I think makes the Program API approach durable at scale.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat

contacts.research_hook: Bengaluru-based SWE building at PromptQL; the blog post 'Killing Slack was the only way to make AI accurate' (April 2026) captures what the core eng team is solving -- accuracy through structured data pipelines rather than prompt engineering
contacts.subject: April post on Slack vs accurate AI
contacts.content:
Hi Rohan,

Going through PromptQL's blog this week and the April post on Slack vs AI accuracy put words to something I keep running into in production: context fragmented across chat threads breaks retrieval accuracy. The systems that work are the ones where data structure and retrieval architecture match the actual query patterns.

I've spent two years building that kind of system. At Vivansh: a permission-aware RAG chatbot on Milvus, 10K+ content chunks, 2-stage retrieval with 250+ per-query access control checks, GPT-4o for query rewriting and answer generation, 80% MRR. At Silver Touch I rebuilt a 50K-queries/month RAG system from FAISS to Milvus, 45% to 95% MRR, after testing 6 embedding models on production data.

I'm based in Ahmedabad and tracking what PromptQL is building. If the Bengaluru team is hiring, happy to share more. Would you be open to pointing me in the right direction?

Amit
amitgohel.dev | github.com/amit-gohel | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: April post on Slack vs accurate AI
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Rohan,

Following up in case this got buried. Since my last note I read PromptQL's 'Your Homegrown AI Is Already Dead' post from last week. The 18-month failure pattern it describes matches what I saw at Silver Touch before the RAG infrastructure rebuild: the system worked in demos but fell apart on real query distributions. That's the problem I want to be working on.

Amit

contacts.subject: AI/ML engineer for PromptQL's Bengaluru team
contacts.content:
Hi Sushma,

Reaching out because I think my background is a clean match for what PromptQL is hiring for on the AI engineering side.

Two years of production Python-based AI: RAG chatbots at 95% MRR handling 50K queries per month, multi-tenant permission-aware retrieval on Milvus, multi-model LLM orchestration across GPT-4o, Anthropic Claude, and Gemini 2.5 Pro, and on-premise document processing pipelines for sensitive data. Current role is AI Engineer at Vivansh Infotech in Ahmedabad; prior research stint at IIT Delhi. Microsoft Azure AI Engineer Associate certified.

Resume and live projects: amitgohel.dev

If there's an open AI engineering role on the Bengaluru team I should look at, I'd appreciate a pointer. If not, happy to stay on your radar for when something opens up.

Thanks,
Amit
